open_gaps.SEC-002: LOW (unchanged). Still no numPages>1000 cap at viewer.js loadDocument() (single viewer-load boundary) and no cumulative-queued-bytes cap at the multi-file ingest boundaries (merge.js queued PDFs, img2pdf.js queued images). This tick's two new modules (permissions.js TASK-362, js-inspector.js TASK-364) are BOTH read-only inspectors that touch ONLY the already-validated open doc (pdf.js getPermissions()/getData() + pdf-lib structural read with ignoreEncryption:true) and upload/download/mutate nothing -> NEITHER is a new ingest boundary. Same single fix (page-count cap once at viewer load + cumulative-bytes caps in merge.js/img2pdf.js addFiles()). Developer task, not security's to implement.
open_gaps.SEC-JS-INFLATE: LOW (NEW this tick). js-inspector.js (TASK-364) inflates FlateDecode /JS streams FULLY into memory via the browser DecompressionStream before truncating to MAX_SNIPPET=600, and collectNameTree walks the /Names /JavaScript tree with NO cap on the number of script entries (only a depth-50 recursion guard). A crafted decompression-bomb /JS stream — or many flate entries — could spike tab memory on the 1.6 GiB box. Self-inflicted (uploader's own already-<=50 MB validated open doc, single tab, no server impact) -> same LOW self-DoS family as SEC-002. Recommended developer hardening: cap inflated bytes (abort stream past ~1-2 MB) + cap script-entry count. NOT XSS/exec — all extracted source rendered via <pre>.textContent only, never DOM-injected, never eval'd, action targets never followed.
open_gaps.SEC-CSP-FRAME: INFO. frame-src 'self' blob: in the live CSP (added 2026-06-13 for the Print tool). Allows only same-origin + same-origin-blob frames, no remote origins; frame-ancestors 'self' unchanged. Verified live this tick.

notes: 55 JS modules (+2 since the 2026-06-14 04:30 review: permissions.js TASK-362, js-inspector.js TASK-364). BOTH CLEAN, no new XSS/exec/network findings. Both are read-only inspectors on the already-validated open doc (NOT ingest boundaries, nothing uploaded/downloaded/mutated). permissions.js: every PDF value (/Filter handler, V/R) via textContent (innerHTML only for empty-string clears); pdf.js getPermissions() + pdf-lib /Encrypt structural read. js-inspector.js is the security-sensitive one (extracts embedded script source) and is safe by construction: source/names/action targets via textContent + <pre>.textContent only, truncated to 600 chars, never DOM-injected/eval'd/run, URI/Launch/GoToR targets shown as inert plain text, no <a href>. ONE new LOW sub-surface SEC-JS-INFLATE (see open_gaps): full in-memory FlateDecode inflate + uncapped script-entry count = self-DoS amplifier on the 1.6 GiB box, same LOW family, developer hardening recommended. New-file audit: zero real innerHTML/outerHTML/insertAdjacentHTML/document.write/eval/new Function/fetch/XHR/sendBeacon/<a href> sinks across both (only innerHTML='' clears); zero external requests in both + app.js.
